subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Logical sequence with clear introduction, body and partial conclusion
Well-structured table of contents and sign-posting through headings and pagination
Effective use of figure captions and equation numbering
weaknesses: Several planned sections remain âTBCâ or incomplete (cone optimisation, full conclusion)
Background paragraphs contain material of limited mathematical relevance
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission demonstrates a coherent and logically developed structure with clear sign-posting and organization (Band 3). Occasional digressions, unfinished sections and some irrelevant background prevent it reaching the highest band, but overall the presentation is well organized and concise enough to merit 3 marks.

strengths: Generally consistent use of correct calculus notation and units
Effective combination of text, formulas, tables and diagrams as representations
Step-by-step derivations numbered and explained, supporting logical clarity
weaknesses: Minor notation slips (e.g. âx2â vs âxÂ²â, inconsistent V casing)
Omission of some intermediate algebraic steps reduces absolute clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical language and notation are mostly consistent and intelligible, with effective multiple representations and good logical communication (Band 3). Minor errors and the absence of certain representations keep this at 3 marks rather than the top band.

strengths: Clear personal context linking cosmetics optimisation to the authorâs daily routine and sustainability interests
Evidence of independent decisions in selecting and measuring real cosmetic items
Creative future research suggestions (3D modelling, FEA) show initiative
weaknesses: Methodology remains standard calculus application without deeper originality
Cone section is unfinished, limiting the demonstration of sustained personal insight
Creativity is good but stops short of exceptional innovation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows significant independent thinking, a clear personal approach and good creativity (Band 2). It does not reach the outstanding level in any strand, so 2 marks appropriately reflect the quality of personal engagement.

strengths: Meaningful discussion of assumptions, measurement errors and practical challenges
Thoughtful analysis of simplifications and their impact on results
Clear evaluation of advantages, disadvantages and future implications
weaknesses: Reflection is largely confined to one section rather than woven throughout
Analysis is thoughtful but lacks deep, data-driven insight
Evaluation remains somewhat speculative with limited quantitative backing
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is meaningful reflection and thoughtful analysis of outcomes, matching the descriptor for Band 2. The reflection is neither superficial nor substantially critical throughout, so 2 marks best capture its quality.

strengths: Correct application of SL-level calculus to optimise two solid shapes, with clear derivations
Mathematics employed is relevant, commensurate with course level and effectively models the problem
Demonstrates thorough understanding in the completed cylinder and cuboid sections
weaknesses: Several algebraic and notation errors (mis-typed exponents, variable casing)
Incomplete derivation and second-derivative test for the cone section
Inconsistent units conversion (ml to cmÂ³) noted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the cone optimisation is incomplete and some minor errors occur, the student applies relevant SL mathematics comprehensively across two full cases, shows good level appropriateness and a high degree of understanding. On balance this merits 5 marks, reflecting effective and well-applied mathematics just below the top band.

strandSelections.reasoning: Derivations are presented step-by-step, numbered and explained, giving a logical thread that readers can follow.  Occasional algebraic mistakes and the unfinished cone section detract from clarity, so the communication is good but not excellent.  Band 3 therefore best describes the work.

The student shows understanding of differentiation and links first- and second-derivative tests to minima.  Nevertheless, there are several algebraic/notation errors (e.g. surface-area formula mistyped, optimal surface area for cylinder mis-calculated, inconsistent variables).  These indicate âsome understanding with errorsâ.  Band 2 is therefore suitable.

The student includes labelled diagrams of each solid, a variables table, algebraic derivations and numerical calculations.  Although no graphs are provided, the combination of textual explanation, formulae and images constitutes effective multiple representation.  The level of effectiveness matches band 3 rather than the âhighly effectiveâ top band.

The exploration follows a logical sequence: introduction, derivation of general formulae, application to specific cosmetics, and a reflective section.  Headings, equation numbering and a table of contents guide the reader effectively, so the work remains easy to follow.  Coherence is weakened by the unfinished cone section and occasional digressions, yet the argument still develops sensibly from problem statement to partial conclusion.  Overall, the submission meets the descriptor for a coherent and logically developed report but falls short of the âhighly coherentâ standard.

Linking calculus to environmental waste and creating real-world applications exhibits creativity and initiative beyond a purely theoretical task.  Nonetheless, only two shapes are fully worked and no advanced techniques or original models are attempted, so the level is âgoodâ rather than âexceptionalâ.  Band 2 fits.

The student analyses why simplifications may skew results and considers practical challenges such as internal seals and consumer behaviour.  While thoughtful, the analysis is not penetrating or data-supported, thus corresponding to âthoughtfulâ rather than âdeep and insightfulâ.  Band 2 is appropriate.

Advantages, disadvantages and future research suggestions indicate a clear evaluation of outcomes and implications.  However, quantitative assessment of how optimisation affects waste is limited and some conclusions remain speculative.  The strand fits the âclear evaluationâ descriptor (band 2).

The evaluation section discusses assumptions, measurement error and irregular shapes, showing meaningful reflection on limitations.  Reflection is confined largely to one section rather than woven throughout, so depth is adequate but not âsubstantialâ.  Band 2 therefore matches the evidence.

Symbols, calculus notation and units are generally correct and employed throughout.  Minor slips (e.g. âx2â instead of âxÂ²â, inconsistent capitalisation of V) show that usage is not flawless, yet the majority of symbols, derivative notation and equations are appropriate and intelligible.  This corresponds to âmostly consistent useâ (band 3).

The exploration is framed through the studentâs morning routine, sustainability interest and retail experience, demonstrating a clear personal approach.  However, the methodology itself remains conventional, so the work does not reach the âhighly personal and innovativeâ standard.  Band 2 is justified.

Most material is relevant to the optimisation task, but lengthy background paragraphs on SDGs, retail experience and marketing add little mathematical value.  Repetition of aims and extended environmental commentary reduce conciseness.  Therefore the work is âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elements,â fitting band 2.

Optimisation of surface area for fixed volume, use of derivatives and second derivative tests are fully relevant to the aim.  Mathematics is applied appropriately, yet remains standard SL content and is incomplete for cones, so the work is ârelevant and appropriateâ rather than âeffectiveâ or âcomprehensiveâ.  Band 3 best fits.

Selecting personal cosmetic items, measuring them and tailoring optimisation to those products show independent decisions beyond textbook examples.  Nevertheless, the mathematical approach (standard calculus minimisation) is routine and the cone section is unfinished, limiting evidence of deeper originality.  Hence âsignificant evidenceâ (band 2) is appropriate.

A clear introduction, body and evaluation are provided, reinforced by subsection headings and a useful table of contents.  Pagination and figure captions add further sign-posting.  However, several planned sections (cone optimisation, full conclusion) remain incomplete, so the structure, while clear, is not exceptional.  Hence the strand aligns with the âwell-organised with clear structureâ band rather than the top band.

The calculus techniques and optimisation problems are exactly at SL AA level.  No material falls below the course level, but the exploration does not extend beyond it.  Hence the mathematics is âcommensurate with course levelâ (band 3).

annotations.comment: The title is clear, specific and personally engaging, effectively orienting the reader to the context and goal of optimizing makeup packaging.
The table of contents is well structured and clearly guides the reader through each section, enhancing navigability.
Several entries remain marked âTBCâ in the contents; finalizing these section titles and page references will improve overall organization.
Background paragraphs are rich in context but contain limited mathematical relevance; consider condensing to focus on the problemâs mathematical aspects.
The personal interest section effectively links the authorâs experience, SDG awareness and MUN involvement, showcasing genuine engagement.
The stated aims are too broad; refine them with explicit, measurable objectives (e.g. minimize surface area for given volume) and define key variables.
Assumptions are thoughtfully listed and contribute to methodological transparency, preparing the reader for simplifications made later.
Table 1 comprehensively defines all variables and notation in one place, greatly aiding mathematical communication.
Assumption 3 notes irregular shapes but lacks a quantitative estimate of how much this deviates from regular-shape calculations; consider estimating that error.
Equation 1.0 uses âx2â and âxyâ instead of proper exponent notation; revise to $$A=2x^2+4xh$$ to reflect consistent mathematical symbols.
When introducing Volume (Eqn 1.1), remind the reader that $$l=x$$ for clarity when replacing variables.
In Eqn 1.4 the fraction is recorded as $$\frac{v}{x2}$$; correct to $$\frac{V}{x^2}$$ and maintain uppercase V.
Be consistent with the casing of variable V throughout differentiation and substitution to prevent confusion.
The second derivative is labelled $$\frac{d^2A}{dr^2}$$ in the cuboid section but the independent variable is x; correct notation to $$\frac{d^2A}{dx^2}$$.
The derivation logically and clearly leads to the optimal condition $$x=h$$, demonstrating correct use of calculus to minimize surface area.
Volume is given in ml but surface area uses cmÂ²; explicitly note that 1 ml = 1 cmÂ³ and convert before calculation.
Reported surface area 109.89 cmÂ² should reflect the precision of measurements (e.g. two significant figures), so consider rounding accordingly.
The cylinder optimization derivation is presented step-by-step with clear labeling, supporting logical clarity.
After substituting Eqn 2.3 into Eqn 2.1, the intermediate expression is omitted; include that algebraic step for full clarity.
The second derivative evaluation yields 12Ï but the correct value is 8Ï; re-derive to maintain accuracy.
The cone optimization section is left incomplete; extend the derivation by differentiating and applying the second derivative test.
Introduce and define the slant height relation $$l=\sqrt{r^2+h^2}$$ before differentiating the coneâs surface area for clarity.
Reflection content is clustered in one section; weaving brief reflective comments after each application would deepen critical analysis.
Future research suggestions are creative and show initiative, particularly the proposal to use 3D modeling and FEA for more accurate optimization.
The conclusion restates findings but lacks concrete, data-driven recommendations (e.g. specific shape ratios); consider adding actionable suggestions.
Bibliography entries exhibit inconsistent formatting and capitalization; adopting a single citation style (e.g. APA) will enhance presentation.
